summary: Draft, unmerged SObjLib decomp PR. It matched `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`, added a large mostly-matching implementation of `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68`, and corrected `HSD_SObj` color fields from individual bytes to `GXColor`. The decomp.dev bot reported one new full match and a major partial improvement, but also a small `HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC` regression plus data-section regressions. The PR was closed after being superseded by PR #2485.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: added `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`, replaced the `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68` placeholder with a large GX/TEV renderer implementation, added includes, local structs, and packed color constants.
Header typing update: replaced byte fields at offsets 0x38-0x3F with `GXColor x38` and `GXColor x3C`; changed `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` priority parameter to `u8`.
Callsite adjustment for the new `GXColor` struct fields in `fn_801A6664`, changing raw byte writes to `.r/.g/.b/.a` component access.

what_changed: Implemented `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`, replacing a `/// #HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` placeholder. The new code allocates an `HSD_SObj`, asserts allocation, initializes texture/TLUT objects with `GXInitTlutObj`, `GXInitTexObjCI`, or `GXInitTexObj`, optionally initializes a second texture when `use_x8` is set, initializes SObj defaults, computes UV bounds with `GXGetTexObjWidth` and `GXGetTexObjHeight`, and links the SObj via `HSD_SObjLib_803A44D4`.

Replaced the `/// #HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68` placeholder with a long GX drawing routine that configures vertex descriptors, Z texture state, culling, TEV stages, TLUT/texture loads, alpha/blend modes, emits a textured quad through the GP FIFO, and restores HSD render state.
Changed `struct HSD_SObj` color storage from eight separate `u8` fields (`x38` through `x3F`) into two packed `GXColor` fields, `x38` and `x3C`.
Updated `gmregtyfall.c` to use `temp_r3->x3C.a` and `temp_r3_2->x3C.r/g/b/a` instead of raw byte fields.
Adjusted `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`'s priority argument from `int` to `u8` in both source and header.
Added `tobj.h`, `baselib/debug.h`, `baselib/pobj.h`, and `<MSL/trigf.h>` includes for texture descriptors, assertions, GX-related data, and `sinf`/`cosf`.
Converted several color constants from extern `HSD_SObjLib_804DEA80`-style declarations to file-local `static s32 lbl_804DEA80` through `lbl_804DEA90` definitions.
Removed duplicate `@todo` comments on `HSD_SObjLib_803A44D4` and removed a `data_pad_jumptable` placeholder.
The PR was closed unmerged; a final comment says it was superseded by `https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2485`.
smart_moves: Modeling `HSD_SObj` color blocks as `GXColor` made the code align with GX APIs such as `GXSetTevColor` and turned ambiguous byte writes into clear `.r/.g/.b/.a` assignments.
The `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` implementation preserved narrow argument behavior with `u8 priority` and `(u8) use_x8` checks, which likely helped the function reach a 100% match per the bot report.
The constructor code handled both CI/TLUT and non-TLUT texture paths explicitly, matching the two GX initialization families rather than forcing a single abstraction.

The renderer restored global render state at the end with `HSD_StateInvalidate`, `HSD_StateInitTev`, `HSD_ClearVtxDesc`, and `HSD_StateSetZMode`, which is important for baselib draw routines that leave GX state dirty.
The TEV color setup used packed `s32` constants copied through stack temporaries and cast to `GXColor`/`GXColorS10`, a useful tactic when the original code treats color data as packed words.
Keeping offset-style temporary structs such as `SObjLibSObjDesc` let the decomp express descriptor layout without overclaiming semantic names.
decomp_lessons: When a struct is really carrying GX colors, prefer `GXColor` fields over four independent bytes; then update every caller to component access so the header and use sites remain consistent.

Small type-width corrections in function prototypes can matter for matching. This PR changed the `priority` argument of `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` from `int` to `u8` and used explicit `(u8)` casts in flag checks.
Rendering functions may need direct GX state sequencing rather than helper abstractions; the diff preserves detailed `GXSetTev*`, `GXSetBlendMode`, `GXSetZTexture`, and FIFO-write order.
Unsigned byte-range tests such as `(u8) (temp_r29 - 8) <= 1U` are worth preserving when matching compiler output for format checks.
Data-section layout is fragile: the bot reported code gains but also `.data` and `.sbss` regressions, so static/extern constant and table changes need section-level verification.
Do not treat this PR as the accepted final version without checking PR #2485, because this one was explicitly superseded.
naming_conventions: Unknown baselib functions remained in address-suffixed form, e.g. `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` and `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68`.
Offset-based struct member names were retained where semantics were still unclear, e.g. `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x38`, and `x3C`.
Packed color fields use `GXColor` member names and then `.r`, `.g`, `.b`, `.a` for channel access at call sites.
File-local anonymous constants in this diff used `lbl_804DEA80`-style address labels rather than exported `HSD_SObjLib_804DEA80` names.
The introduced local descriptor type was named `SObjLibSObjDesc` and kept offset members for `HSD_ImageDesc*` and `HSD_TlutDesc*`; there was no reviewer feedback confirming these names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `u8 priority` and `(u8) use_x8 != 0` in `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C` to preserve narrow comparison behavior.
Used `PAD_STACK(0x10)` plus explicit stack locals `sp50` through `sp74` in `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68`, suggesting stack layout was part of the matching attempt.
Copied packed static `s32` color constants into stack locals before casting to `GXColor` or `GXColorS10` for `GXSetTevKColor` and `GXSetTevColorS10`.
Grouped `GXGetTexObjFmt` switch cases by raw format values: `0/1`, `2/3`, `4`, and `5/6/8/9/10/14`.
Loaded TLUTs using the compact unsigned check `(u8) (temp_r29 - 8) <= 1U`.
Emitted vertices and texture coordinates with volatile writes to `0xCC008000` after `GXBegin(GX_QUADS, GX_VTXFMT0, 4U)`.
Used `<MSL/trigf.h>` `sinf` and `cosf` calls with `0.5F` factors for rotated quad coordinate computation.
Bot evidence says the tactics fully matched `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`, while `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68` only reached 97.28% in this PR.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 66.48%, up 0.01% and +372 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported one new full match: `main/sysdolphin/baselib/sobjlib` `HSD_SObjLib_803A477C`, +612 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
decomp.dev bot reported `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68` improved by +2618 bytes from 0.00% to 97.28%, with `.sdata2` and `.sdata` also improving.
decomp.dev bot also reported one broken match: `HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC`, 100.00% to 99.95%, plus `.data` and `.sbss` regressions.
Author comment: `regression will be fixed when linking`.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2485 for the accepted or later superseding SObjLib solution.
Check whether `HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC` and the `.data`/`.sbss` regressions were resolved after linking or in PR #2485.
Search current code for `HSD_SObj` raw byte color accesses to ensure all callers use `GXColor` component fields.
Compare current `HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68` against this 97.28% draft to see which GX/TEV details finished the match.
Verify whether the `lbl_804DEA80` through `lbl_804DEA90` static constants remained file-local or were renamed/reorganized in the final merged work.
